login.title: Log In
login.noAccount: Sign in with your EULEX account. Don't have one?
login.legalNotice: By signing in, you accept the <terms>Terms of Service</terms> and <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy> on eulex.ai and confirm that you are 18 years of age or older.

signup.title: Create Account
signup.managedByEulex: Account registration is managed through EULEX
signup.createOnEulex: Create your free account on eulex.ai, then come back here to sign in.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.description: Permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.
danger.confirmMessage: Are you sure? This will permanently delete your account.

alerts.failedDeleteAccount: Failed to delete account. Please try again.

models.title: Model Preferences
models.localLlmNote: LocalLLM models are configured by the server administrator and are available to all users.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: You must provide your own API keys for the app to work or add your API keys into the .env file if you are running your own instance of Max.
apiKeys.titleGenNote: Title generation automatically routes to the cheapest model of whichever provider you've configured (LocalLLM Lite if available, otherwise Gemini Flash Lite, otherwise Claude Haiku).
apiKeys.addKeyTooltip: Add a {provider} API key to use this model
apiKeys.sharedKeyPlaceholder: Using shared key — paste your own to override
apiKeys.sharedKeyHint: Max will use a shared key configured by the administrator. You only need to paste your own if you want to use a separate account.

connectors.title: Connectors
connectors.description: Connectors plug external tools into Max via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) — legal-data sources, web research, internal company APIs, and so on. Tools discovered from each connector become available to the chat assistant.

trustWarning.title: Only add connectors you trust
trustWarning.description: A connector's operator can see anything Max sends in tool calls — your prompts, document excerpts, and the tool's own response. Custom headers (including Authorization tokens) are sent on every request.

addForm.name: Name
addForm.apiKeyHeadersDesc: For servers that accept a static token. You paste it as a custom header below.
addForm.oauthDiscoverDesc: For spec-conformant servers. You'll sign in via a popup — no token to paste.
addForm.customHeadersHint: Sent on every request. Common usage: Authorization → Bearer <token>.

card.nameContainedSecret: Name contained what looks like a secret — displayed redacted. Edit the server to fix.
card.builtinHint: Preset connector — you can disable it, but the configuration is managed by the administrator.

confirm.removeMessage: Are you sure you want to remove "{name}"? This also deletes any saved sign-in tokens.
confirm.resetOauthTitle: Reset OAuth and sign in again
confirm.resetOauthMessage: This wipes saved tokens and re-registers Max with "{name}" from scratch. Use when the connector is stuck on "Client Not Registered" or refuses to refresh. You'll be asked to sign in again.

alerts.noAuthorizeUrl: Authorization server did not return a URL.
alerts.popupBlockedMessage: Couldn't open the sign-in window — check your popup blocker and try again.

assistant.disclaimer: AI can make mistakes. Answers are not legal advice.

modelToggle.triggerAria: Choose model and reasoning effort. Currently {model}.
modelToggle.apiKeyMissingTitle: API key missing for selected model

effort.label: Reasoning effort
effort.lowTitle: Faster, cheaper. Minimal thinking before responding.
effort.mediumTitle: Balanced. A moderate amount of thinking before responding.
effort.highTitle: Deepest thinking. Slower and more expensive, best on hard problems.

creditsExhausted.resetNote: Your message credits automatically reset on the first day of each month.

deleteChats.confirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete all {count} {count, plural, one {chat} other {chats}}? This action is permanent and cannot be undone.

people.couldntAdd: Couldn't add the member. Try again.
people.couldntRemove: Couldn't remove the member. Try again.

shareChat.title: Share Chat
shareChat.explainer: Invite people by email. They'll receive a snapshot of this conversation; once they sign in with the same email they can join the chat and continue it with you.
shareChat.none: No one has been invited yet.
shareChat.inviteSent: {count, plural, one {Invite sent.} other {# invites sent.}}
shareChat.errorSendFailed: Couldn't send the invite. Try again.
shareChat.continueExplainer: Continue the conversation together — you'll be added as a collaborator.
shareChat.errorEmailMismatchTitle: This invite is bound to a different email
shareChat.errorEmailMismatchBody: Sign in with the email address the invitation was sent to.
shareChat.errorEmailMismatchBodyWithEmail: Sign in with {email} to view this conversation.
shareChat.errorExpiredTitle: This share link is no longer active
shareChat.errorExpiredBody: Ask the sender to share the conversation again.
shareChat.errorNotFoundBody: The link may have been mistyped or the share was deleted.

documents.ownerOnlyDelete: delete these documents — only the document creator can delete a document
documents.ownerOnlyDeletePartial: delete {count} of the selected documents — only the document creator can delete a document

confirmDelete.reviewBody: Are you sure you want to delete this tabular review? This cannot be undone.
confirmDelete.reviewBodyNamed: Are you sure you want to delete the review "{title}"? This cannot be undone.
confirmDelete.reviewsBody: Are you sure you want to delete {count} {count, plural, one {review} other {reviews}}? This cannot be undone.
confirmDelete.connectorBody: Are you sure you want to disconnect {name}? Max won't be able to access files in that account again until you reconnect. Already-imported files stay in Max.

empty.allDesc: Automate document analysis with reusable prompts and tabular review templates.

streaming.resolvedChangesAcrossDocs: {changeCount} resolved tracked {changeCount, plural, one {change} other {changes}} across {docCount} {docCount, plural, one {document} other {documents}}

wordAddin.title: Word Add-in
wordAddin.description: Use Max directly inside Microsoft Word. The add-in connects to this account so your projects, chats, and uploads stay in sync.

pair.title: 1. Generate a pairing code
pair.description: Each code is valid for 5 minutes and can be redeemed only once. After a Word add-in is paired, the access stays active until you sign out from Word.

install.title: 2. Install the add-in in Word
install.step2: In Word, open the Insert tab, click Add-ins → Get Add-ins → Upload My Add-in.
install.step3: Select the downloaded manifest.xml. The Max sidebar will appear on the Home tab.
install.step4: Open the Max pane and paste the 6-digit pairing code when prompted.

use.title: 3. Start working
use.description: Once paired you can chat with Max, attach project documents, request track-change suggestions, and let the assistant comment on a selection — all without leaving Word.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

changelog.title: What's new
changelog.description: Recent features, improvements, and fixes in Max. Newest entries at the top.

connectorsPage.title: File sources
connectorsPage.description: Connect Google Drive, OneDrive, or Box to import documents directly into Max. Files are copied into Max at import time — later changes in the source don't modify the imported copy.
connectorsPage.empty: No file connectors are available right now. Contact your administrator.
connectorsPage.contactAdmin: The operator has not configured this connector
